paper_title: Dating a tropical ice core by time–frequency analysis of ion concentration depth profiles

methods.method_name: Direct Evaluation of Time Delay Estimates
methods.description: This method evaluates the time delay estimates using spectrogram and scalogram from pre-processed nitrate concentration depth profiles. It aims to determine the concentration of nitrate as a function of time delay estimates using Fourier time-frequency and wavelet transforms.

steps.description: Determine the time delay estimates, t(d), with the spectrogram and the scalogram.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate time delay estimates using spectrogram and scalogram

steps.description: Use a spline transformation to get the concentration of NO3 as a function of the time delay estimate.
steps.searchable_summary: apply spline transformation to obtain nitrate concentration as a function of time delay
outputs: nitrate concentration as a function of time delay

steps.description: Present the spectrograms of S NO3(t) for Fourier time-frequency and wavelet transforms.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize spectrograms for Fourier and wavelet transforms
inputs: nitrate concentration as a function of time delay

steps.description: Check the quality of the method by analyzing the spectrograms for time delay values lower than 160 years.
steps.searchable_summary: validate method by analyzing spectrograms for non-aliased data

methods.description: This method involves pre-processing nitrate concentration data to make it suitable for time-frequency analysis. It includes interpolation, thresholding, and filtering to remove high impulses and ensure regular distribution.

steps.description: Apply thresholding to avoid divergence phenomena by determining the threshold from the histogram of the concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: apply thresholding based on concentration histogram

steps.description: Apply four interpolation methods: linear, nearest neighbour, Hermite polynomial, and spline.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate data using linear, nearest neighbour, Hermite polynomial, and spline methods

steps.description: Remove the mean concentration value after interpolation.
steps.searchable_summary: remove mean concentration value from interpolated data

steps.description: Estimate the averaged concentration spectrum and apply a high-pass filter to eliminate low frequencies.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate concentration spectrum and apply high-pass filter
